title: Debriefing and feedback sessions | CanadaBuys

structure_blocks.heading: Debriefing and feedback sessions
structure_blocks.text: Debriefing and feedback sessions

structure_blocks.heading: Debriefing and feedback sessions
structure_blocks.text: Debriefings and feedback sessions are held to provide information to offerors concerning their offers. The following sections describe the different types of sessions and provide best practices and procedures for each.

structure_blocks.heading: Debriefing
structure_blocks.text: Debriefing

structure_blocks.heading: Debriefing
structure_blocks.text: The primary goal of a debriefing is to explain to offerors why their offers were not selected. This transparency aids in improving their future proposals and demonstrates the fairness, openness and transparency of the federal contracting process. Feedback from offerors can be utilized by buyers to improve future solicitations.

structure_blocks.heading: Procedure for requesting debriefings
structure_blocks.text: Procedure for requesting debriefings

structure_blocks.heading: Procedure for requesting debriefings
structure_blocks.text: Solicitations must clearly state that offerors are entitled to request a debriefing on the outcome of the procurement process.

structure_blocks.heading: Procedure for requesting debriefings
structure_blocks.text: Upon the award of a contract or issuance of a standing offer or supply arrangement, offerors may request a debriefing.

structure_blocks.heading: Procedure for requesting debriefings
structure_blocks.text: In complex procurements involving multiple phases, offerors are notified of their status at the end of each phase and may request detailed feedback at these points.

structure_blocks.heading: Conducting debriefings
structure_blocks.text: Conducting debriefings

structure_blocks.heading: Conducting debriefings
structure_blocks.text: Debriefings are typically conducted in writing through regret letters, which offer detailed feedback. For more comprehensive examples, you may refer to the available samples of regret letters .

structure_blocks.heading: Content of debriefings
structure_blocks.text: Content of debriefings

structure_blocks.heading: Content of debriefings
structure_blocks.text: Within the limits of information disclosure policies, a debriefing will include the following, as applicable:

structure_blocks.heading: Content of debriefings
structure_blocks.text: names of successful offerors

structure_blocks.heading: Content of debriefings
structure_blocks.text: total estimated cost of the contract(s)

structure_blocks.heading: Content of debriefings
structure_blocks.text: total evaluated price of the successful offeror(s) and the total score, if applicable

structure_blocks.heading: Content of debriefings
structure_blocks.text: very general information on the relative strengths of the successful offer(s)/arrangement(s), without divulging any confidential and commercial information

structure_blocks.heading: Content of debriefings
structure_blocks.text: an outline of the reasons that the offer/arrangement was not successful according to evaluation criteria and selection methodology

structure_blocks.heading: Content of debriefings
structure_blocks.text: scores achieved by the offeror on all rated criteria, with sufficient details for the offeror to understand why those scores were assigned

structure_blocks.heading: Feedback session
structure_blocks.text: Feedback session

structure_blocks.heading: Feedback session
structure_blocks.text: A feedback session is a form of debriefing, and is provided upon request when a solicitation is cancelled and planned for reissue. These sessions are designed to provide the offeror with information specific to their offer/arrangement, including which mandatory criteria they passed or failed, or their overall technical score or their score for each technical criterion. This helps offerors to understand why their offer/arrangement failed to meet the requirements. You must not disclose any details about other offers/arrangements or the specifics of the solicitation of offers to be reissued.

structure_blocks.heading: Documentation and fairness
structure_blocks.text: Documentation and fairness

structure_blocks.heading: Documentation and fairness
structure_blocks.text: To ensure the fairness, openness, and transparency of the procurement process, all offerors must have access to the same information on the solicitation of offers to be reissued. Therefore, all information provided in a feedback session must be documented by the Contracting Authority. Where relevant, this feedback is to be provided in a generic form in the reissued solicitation of offers to maintain fairness to all potential offerors.

structure_blocks.heading: Methods of providing feedback
structure_blocks.text: Methods of providing feedback

structure_blocks.heading: Methods of providing feedback
structure_blocks.text: You may deliver feedback through one or both of the following methods:

structure_blocks.heading: Methods of providing feedback
structure_blocks.text: highlighting key areas of concern more prominently in the reissued solicitation of offers with additional instructions, for example by adding text to inform or focus attention

structure_blocks.heading: Methods of providing feedback
structure_blocks.text: organizing an offerors' conference for the solicitation of offers to be reissued

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for choosing feedback method
structure_blocks.text: Considerations for choosing feedback method

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for choosing feedback method
structure_blocks.text: You should consider certain factors when selecting the appropriate method for feedback. These factors include:

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for choosing feedback method
structure_blocks.text: complexity of the procurement or the solicitation documents

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for choosing feedback method
structure_blocks.text: likelihood of another unsuccessful solicitation of offers if no compliant offer/arrangement had been received

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for choosing feedback method
structure_blocks.text: the nature of the issues that necessitated the cancellation and reissuance of the solicitation

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for choosing feedback method
structure_blocks.text: sensitivity and estimated dollar value of the procurement

structure_blocks.heading: Considerations for choosing feedback method
structure_blocks.text: if Treasury Board (TB) contract approval is required, it is mandatory to use both methods of feedback

structure_blocks.heading: Requirements for reissuing solicitations
structure_blocks.text: Requirements for reissuing solicitations

structure_blocks.heading: Requirements for reissuing solicitations
structure_blocks.text: You must include a clause in the reissued solicitation stating that the reissued solicitations cancels and supersedes the previous solicitation, and that a debriefing or feedback session is available upon request to those who submitted an offer in the initial solicitation.

structure_blocks.heading: Requirements for reissuing solicitations
structure_blocks.text: You are to advise offerors not to make any assumptions when preparing their offers for the solicitation to be reissued, as evaluation criteria may change.

structure_blocks.heading: Cancelled solicitations of offers
structure_blocks.text: Cancelled solicitations of offers

structure_blocks.heading: Cancelled solicitations of offers
structure_blocks.text: Feedback for cancelled solicitation of offers must be handled as follows:

structure_blocks.heading: Cancelled solicitations of offers
structure_blocks.text: if a solicitation of offers is cancelled before the closing date, you must follow established procedures for cancelling or reissuing a solicitation

structure_blocks.heading: Cancelled solicitations of offers
structure_blocks.text: if a solicitation of offers is cancelled after the closing date, but before completing the evaluation of mandatory or rated criteria, you must inform all offerors of the cancellation and that the evaluation of offers/arrangements was not completed

structure_blocks.heading: Cancelled solicitations of offers
structure_blocks.text: if a solicitation of offers is cancelled following completion of the evaluation of mandatory or rated criteria and a new solicitation is not planned or will not be issued, a debriefing must be provided upon request

structure_blocks.heading: Cancelled solicitations of offers
structure_blocks.text: if a solicitation of offers is cancelled following the evaluation of mandatory or rated criteria but it is intended to reissue the solicitation of offers, a feedback session will be provided upon request. The specifics of the feedback session are based on the reasons for cancellation

structure_blocks.heading: Significant modifications to requirements
structure_blocks.text: Significant modifications to requirements

structure_blocks.heading: Significant modifications to requirements
structure_blocks.text: If the solicitation of offers is cancelled because all offers/arrangements are non-compliant or do not represent fair value, and the Statement of Requirements for the reissued solicitation is significantly modified, you will inform offerors only of which mandatory criteria or rated criteria requiring a minimum score were failed. But no detail is to be provided as to why the offeror failed each criteria. No information on other rated criteria is to be provided.

structure_blocks.heading: No significant modifications to requirements
structure_blocks.text: No significant modifications to requirements

structure_blocks.heading: No significant modifications to requirements
structure_blocks.text: If the solicitation of offers is cancelled because all offers/arrangements are non-compliant or do not represent fair value and the Statement of Requirements for the reissued solicitation is not significantly modified, you will inform offerors of which mandatory criteria or rated criteria requiring a minimum score were failed. You will also need to include sufficient details for offerors to understand why they failed each criteria. Do not provide any information on other rated criteria.

structure_blocks.heading: Other reasons for cancellation
structure_blocks.text: Other reasons for cancellation

structure_blocks.heading: Other reasons for cancellation
structure_blocks.text: If the solicitation of offers is cancelled for other reasons, you will inform offerors only of which mandatory criteria or rated criteria requiring a minimum score failed. No detail is to be provided as to why the offerors failed each criteria. No information on other rated criteria is to be provided.

structure_blocks.heading: Provision timeline
heading_path: Providing a debriefing or feedback session
structure_blocks.text: A debriefing or feedback session should be conducted within ten working days from the date a request is received.

heading_path: Providing a debriefing or feedback session
structure_blocks.text: Debriefings or feedback sessions can be provided in writing, by telephone, or in person, depending on the most effective method for the specific procurement context. You should accommodate requests for in-person sessions or specific formats, as much as possible.

structure_blocks.text: If a fairness monitor was involved in the evaluation process, you will inform them of the date and time of any telephone or in-person debriefing or feedback session or offerors conference. If legal counsel is to accompany an offeror or participates in any telephone debriefing or feedback session, you must coordinate with Legal Services and discuss their participation.

raw_text:
Debriefing and feedback sessions

Debriefings and feedback sessions are held to provide information to offerors concerning their offers. The following sections describe the different types of sessions and provide best practices and procedures for each.

Debriefing

The primary goal of a debriefing is to explain to offerors why their offers were not selected. This transparency aids in improving their future proposals and demonstrates the fairness, openness and transparency of the federal contracting process. Feedback from offerors can be utilized by buyers to improve future solicitations.

Procedure for requesting debriefings

Solicitations must clearly state that offerors are entitled to request a debriefing on the outcome of the procurement process.

Upon the award of a contract or issuance of a standing offer or supply arrangement, offerors may request a debriefing.

In complex procurements involving multiple phases, offerors are notified of their status at the end of each phase and may request detailed feedback at these points.

Conducting debriefings

Debriefings are typically conducted in writing through regret letters, which offer detailed feedback. For more comprehensive examples, you may refer to the available samples of regret letters .

Content of debriefings

Within the limits of information disclosure policies, a debriefing will include the following, as applicable:

names of successful offerors

total estimated cost of the contract(s)

total evaluated price of the successful offeror(s) and the total score, if applicable

very general information on the relative strengths of the successful offer(s)/arrangement(s), without divulging any confidential and commercial information

an outline of the reasons that the offer/arrangement was not successful according to evaluation criteria and selection methodology

scores achieved by the offeror on all rated criteria, with sufficient details for the offeror to understand why those scores were assigned

Feedback session

A feedback session is a form of debriefing, and is provided upon request when a solicitation is cancelled and planned for reissue. These sessions are designed to provide the offeror with information specific to their offer/arrangement, including which mandatory criteria they passed or failed, or their overall technical score or their score for each technical criterion. This helps offerors to understand why their offer/arrangement failed to meet the requirements. You must not disclose any details about other offers/arrangements or the specifics of the solicitation of offers to be reissued.

Documentation and fairness

To ensure the fairness, openness, and transparency of the procurement process, all offerors must have access to the same information on the solicitation of offers to be reissued. Therefore, all information provided in a feedback session must be documented by the Contracting Authority. Where relevant, this feedback is to be provided in a generic form in the reissued solicitation of offers to maintain fairness to all potential offerors.

Methods of providing feedback

You may deliver feedback through one or both of the following methods:

highlighting key areas of concern more prominently in the reissued solicitation of offers with additional instructions, for example by adding text to inform or focus attention

organizing an offerors' conference for the solicitation of offers to be reissued

Considerations for choosing feedback method

You should consider certain factors when selecting the appropriate method for feedback. These factors include:

complexity of the procurement or the solicitation documents

likelihood of another unsuccessful solicitation of offers if no compliant offer/arrangement had been received

the nature of the issues that necessitated the cancellation and reissuance of the solicitation

sensitivity and estimated dollar value of the procurement

if Treasury Board (TB) contract approval is required, it is mandatory to use both methods of feedback

Requirements for reissuing solicitations

You must include a clause in the reissued solicitation stating that the reissued solicitations cancels and supersedes the previous solicitation, and that a debriefing or feedback session is available upon request to those who submitted an offer in the initial solicitation.

You are to advise offerors not to make any assumptions when preparing their offers for the solicitation to be reissued, as evaluation criteria may change.

Cancelled solicitations of offers

Feedback for cancelled solicitation of offers must be handled as follows:

if a solicitation of offers is cancelled before the closing date, you must follow established procedures for cancelling or reissuing a solicitation

if a solicitation of offers is cancelled after the closing date, but before completing the evaluation of mandatory or rated criteria, you must inform all offerors of the cancellation and that the evaluation of offers/arrangements was not completed

if a solicitation of offers is cancelled following completion of the evaluation of mandatory or rated criteria and a new solicitation is not planned or will not be issued, a debriefing must be provided upon request

if a solicitation of offers is cancelled following the evaluation of mandatory or rated criteria but it is intended to reissue the solicitation of offers, a feedback session will be provided upon request. The specifics of the feedback session are based on the reasons for cancellation

Significant modifications to requirements

If the solicitation of offers is cancelled because all offers/arrangements are non-compliant or do not represent fair value, and the Statement of Requirements for the reissued solicitation is significantly modified, you will inform offerors only of which mandatory criteria or rated criteria requiring a minimum score were failed. But no detail is to be provided as to why the offeror failed each criteria. No information on other rated criteria is to be provided.

No significant modifications to requirements

If the solicitation of offers is cancelled because all offers/arrangements are non-compliant or do not represent fair value and the Statement of Requirements for the reissued solicitation is not significantly modified, you will inform offerors of which mandatory criteria or rated criteria requiring a minimum score were failed. You will also need to include sufficient details for offerors to understand why they failed each criteria. Do not provide any information on other rated criteria.

Other reasons for cancellation

If the solicitation of offers is cancelled for other reasons, you will inform offerors only of which mandatory criteria or rated criteria requiring a minimum score failed. No detail is to be provided as to why the offerors failed each criteria. No information on other rated criteria is to be provided.

Providing a debriefing or feedback session

The following are guidelines and information about debriefings and feedback sessions.

Request timeline

Offerors should request a debriefing or feedback session within fifteen working days of receiving the results of the solicitation process or its cancellation.

Provision timeline

A debriefing or feedback session should be conducted within ten working days from the date a request is received.

Format

Debriefings or feedback sessions can be provided in writing, by telephone, or in person, depending on the most effective method for the specific procurement context. You should accommodate requests for in-person sessions or specific formats, as much as possible.

Consultation with management and legal services

You should consult with management and, if necessary, legal services before providing a debriefing or feedback session for sensitive or high-risk requirements.

Involvement of fairness monitors and legal counsel

If a fairness monitor was involved in the evaluation process, you will inform them of the date and time of any telephone or in-person debriefing or feedback session or offerors conference. If legal counsel is to accompany an offeror or participates in any telephone debriefing or feedback session, you must coordinate with Legal Services and discuss their participation.

Documentation

You must keep thorough records of all debriefing or feedback session information, including minutes or a record of any meeting and any comments or suggestions from offerors.
